title: Online Broker Fee Comparison 2026 — Westmount Fundamentals
description: One broker charges 65¢ per options contract while another charges nothing — but hides it in the spread. We compared 22 brokers side by side.

schemas.headline: Online Broker Fee Comparison 2026
schemas.description: Compare commissions, options fees, margin rates, and features across 22 online brokers in the US and Canada.

mainEntity.name: Which broker has the lowest fees?

acceptedAnswer.text: For stock trading, most major US brokers now offer $0 commissions including Fidelity, Schwab, Robinhood, and Interactive Brokers. For options, Robinhood, Webull, Firstrade, Public, SoFi, moomoo, and Wealthsimple charge $0 per contract. For margin, Interactive Brokers Pro offers the lowest rates starting at 5.14% (BM + 1.5%).

Interactive Brokers Pro has the lowest margin rates in the industry at 5.14% for balances under $100K (BM + 1.5%), dropping further for larger balances. However, for options trading, brokers like Robinhood and Webull offer $0 per-contract fees compared to IBKR's $0.65. IBKR is cheapest overall for active traders who use margin and trade international markets.

mainEntity.name: Do any brokers offer free options trading?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Robinhood, Webull, SoFi, Public, Firstrade, moomoo, and Wealthsimple Trade (Canada) all offer $0 options contracts with no per-contract fee. Note that some of these brokers generate revenue through payment for order flow (PFOF), which can affect execution quality.

mainEntity.name: What is the best broker for beginners?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fidelity and Charles Schwab are consistently rated best for beginners. Both offer $0 stock/ETF trades, extensive educational content, strong customer support, and user-friendly platforms. Schwab offers the thinkorswim platform with paper trading, while Fidelity provides fractional shares and no PFOF for better execution.

mainEntity.name: Which broker has the best margin rates?

acceptedAnswer.text: Interactive Brokers Pro offers the lowest margin rates at 5.14% for balances up to $100K, dropping to 4.64% for $100K–$1M and 4.39% for $1M+. By comparison, Schwab and Fidelity charge over 11%, and E*TRADE charges over 12%. Robinhood Gold offers 6.75%, making it competitive for smaller accounts.
